[33mcommit 37d8ac144c8c8392b0f5d36e130963cf701d2cde[m
Author: mtabebe &lt;michael.abebe@materialize.com&gt;
Date:   Mon Mar 2 16:46:48 2026 -0500

    [adapter] Batch-allocate user IDs to reduce persist writes during DDL
    
    Problem:
    Every DDL statement (CREATE TABLE, CREATE VIEW, etc.) performs a
    persist write + timestamp oracle call to allocate a single user ID.
    This represents one extra round trip in addition to the necessary
    catalog operation for DDL.
    
    Solution:
    - Add &#96;IdPool&#96; struct with &#96;allocate()&#96;, &#96;allocate_many()&#96;, and
      &#96;refill()&#96; methods to pre-allocate user IDs in a contiguous range
    - Add &#96;USER_ID_POOL_BATCH_SIZE&#96; dyncfg (default 512) to control
      batch size
    - Add &#96;user_id_pool&#96; field to &#96;Coordinator&#96; initialized as empty
    - Add Coordinator wrapper methods &#96;allocate_user_id()&#96; and
      &#96;allocate_user_ids(count)&#96; that draw from the pool and auto-refill
      from catalog when exhausted
    - Replace all call sites that used the two-line pattern of
      &#96;get_catalog_write_ts()&#96; + &#96;catalog().allocate_user_id(ts)&#96; with
      the new pooled methods
    
    Testing:
    - New &#96;IdPool&#96; unit tests covering empty pool, single/batch
      allocation, refill, mixed allocation, and invalid range panic
    - New SLT regression test (&#96;test/sqllogictest/id_pool.slt&#96;)
      exercising tables, views, materialized views, indexes, types,
      secrets, and drop/recreate cycles

[32m+[m[32m/// A pool of pre-allocated user IDs to avoid per-DDL persist writes.[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// IDs in the range &#96;[next, upper)&#96; are available for allocation.[m
[32m+[m[32m/// When exhausted, the pool must be refilled via the catalog.[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// # Correctness[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// The pool is owned by [&#96;Coordinator&#96;], which processes all requests[m
[32m+[m[32m/// on a single-threaded event loop. Because every access requires[m
[32m+[m[32m/// &#96;&amp;mut self&#96; on the coordinator, there is no concurrent access to the[m
[32m+[m[32m/// pool — no additional synchronization is needed.[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// Global ID uniqueness is guaranteed because each refill calls[m
[32m+[m[32m/// [&#96;Catalog::allocate_user_ids&#96;], which performs a durable persist[m
[32m+[m[32m/// write that atomically reserves the entire batch before any IDs from[m
[32m+[m[32m/// it are handed out. If the process crashes after a refill but before[m
[32m+[m[32m/// all pre-allocated IDs are consumed, the unused IDs form harmless[m
[32m+[m[32m/// gaps in the sequence — user IDs are not required to be contiguous.[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// This guarantee holds even if multiple &#96;environmentd&#96; processes run[m
[32m+[m[32m/// concurrently. Each process has its own independent pool,[m
[32m+[m[32m/// but every refill goes through the shared persist-backed catalog,[m
[32m+[m[32m/// which serializes allocations across all callers. Two processes[m
[32m+[m[32m/// will therefore never receive overlapping ID ranges,[m
[32m+[m[32m/// for the same reason they could not before this pool existed.[m
[32m+[m[32m#[derive(Debug)][m
[32m+[m[32mpub(crate) struct IdPool {[m
[32m+[m[32m    next: u64,[m
[32m+[m[32m    upper: u64,[m
[32m+[m[32m}[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mimpl IdPool {[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Creates an empty pool.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn empty() -&gt; Self {[m
[32m+[m[32m        IdPool { next: 0, upper: 0 }[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Allocates a single ID from the pool, returning &#96;None&#96; if exhausted.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn allocate(&amp;mut self) -&gt; Option&lt;u64&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        if self.next &lt; self.upper {[m
[32m+[m[32m            let id = self.next;[m
[32m+[m[32m            self.next += 1;[m
[32m+[m[32m            Some(id)[m
[32m+[m[32m        } else {[m
[32m+[m[32m            None[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Allocates &#96;n&#96; consecutive IDs from the pool, returning &#96;None&#96; if[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// insufficient IDs remain.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn allocate_many(&amp;mut self, n: u64) -&gt; Option&lt;Vec&lt;u64&gt;&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        if self.remaining() &gt;= n {[m
[32m+[m[32m            let ids = (self.next..self.next + n).collect();[m
[32m+[m[32m            self.next += n;[m
[32m+[m[32m            Some(ids)[m
[32m+[m[32m        } else {[m
[32m+[m[32m            None[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Returns the number of IDs remaining in the pool.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn remaining(&amp;self) -&gt; u64 {[m
[32m+[m[32m        self.upper - self.next[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Refills the pool with the given range &#96;[next, upper)&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn refill(&amp;mut self, next: u64, upper: u64) {[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert!(next &lt;= upper, "invalid pool range: {next}..{upper}");[m
[32m+[m[32m        self.next = next;[m
[32m+[m[32m        self.upper = upper;[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m[32m}[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m
 #[derive(Debug)][m
 pub enum Message {[m
     Command(OpenTelemetryContext, Command),[m
[35m@@ -1909,6 +1985,9 @@[m [mpub struct Coordinator {[m

[32m+[m[32m    /// Refills the user ID pool by allocating IDs from the catalog.[m
[32m+[m[32m    ///[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Requests &#96;max(min_count, batch_size)&#96; IDs so the pool is never[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// under-filled relative to the configured batch size.[m
[32m+[m[32m    async fn refill_user_id_pool(&amp;mut self, min_count: u64) -&gt; Result&lt;(), AdapterError&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let batch_size = USER_ID_POOL_BATCH_SIZE.get(self.catalog().system_config().dyncfgs());[m
[32m+[m[32m        let to_allocate = std::cmp::max(min_count, u64::from(batch_size));[m
[32m+[m[32m        let id_ts = self.get_catalog_write_ts().await;[m
[32m+[m[32m        let ids = self.catalog().allocate_user_ids(to_allocate, id_ts).await?;[m
[32m+[m[32m        if let (Some((first_id, _)), Some((last_id, _))) = (ids.first(), ids.last()) {[m
[32m+[m[32m            let start = match first_id {[m
[32m+[m[32m                CatalogItemId::User(id) =&gt; *id,[m
[32m+[m[32m                other =&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m                    return Err(AdapterError::Internal(format!([m
[32m+[m[32m                        "expected User CatalogItemId, got {other:?}"[m
[32m+[m[32m                    )));[m
[32m+[m[32m                }[m
[32m+[m[32m            };[m
[32m+[m[32m            let end = match last_id {[m
[32m+[m[32m                CatalogItemId::User(id) =&gt; *id + 1, // exclusive upper bound[m
[32m+[m[32m                other =&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m                    return Err(AdapterError::Internal(format!([m
[32m+[m[32m                        "expected User CatalogItemId, got {other:?}"[m
[32m+[m[32m                    )));[m
[32m+[m[32m                }[m
[32m+[m[32m            };[m
[32m+[m[32m            self.user_id_pool.refill(start, end);[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m        Ok(())[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Allocates a single user ID, refilling the pool from the catalog if needed.[m
[32m+[m[32m    async fn allocate_user_id(&amp;mut self) -&gt; Result&lt;(CatalogItemId, GlobalId), AdapterError&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        if let Some(id) = self.user_id_pool.allocate() {[m
[32m+[m[32m            return Ok((CatalogItemId::User(id), GlobalId::User(id)));[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m        self.refill_user_id_pool(1).await?;[m
[32m+[m[32m        let id = self.user_id_pool.allocate().expect("ID pool just refilled");[m
[32m+[m[32m        Ok((CatalogItemId::User(id), GlobalId::User(id)))[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Allocates &#96;count&#96; user IDs, refilling the pool from the catalog if needed.[m
[32m+[m[32m    async fn allocate_user_ids([m
[32m+[m[32m        &amp;mut self,[m
[32m+[m[32m        count: u64,[m
[32m+[m[32m    ) -&gt; Result&lt;Vec&lt;(CatalogItemId, GlobalId)&gt;, AdapterError&gt; {[m

[32m+[m[32m    }[m

[32m+[m

[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_allocate_many() {[m

[32m+[m[32m        // But 2 works.[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(2), Some(vec![103, 104]));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 0);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_allocate_many_zero() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(1, 5);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(0), Some(vec![]));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 4);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_refill_resets_pool() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(0, 2);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(0));[m
[32m+[m[32m        // Refill before exhaustion replaces the range.[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(50, 52);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(50));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(51));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), None);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_mixed_allocate_and_allocate_many() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(0, 10);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(0));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(3), Some(vec![1, 2, 3]));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(4));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 5);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[test][m
[32m+[m[32m    #[should_panic(expected = "invalid pool range")][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_refill_invalid_range_panics() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(10, 5);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m[32m}[m

[35m@@ -0,0 +1,125 @@[m
[32m+[m[32m# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.[m
[32m+[m[32m#[m

[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Regression test for pre-allocated user ID pool.[m
[32m+[m[32m# Exercises multiple DDL types to verify IDs are unique and objects[m
[32m+[m[32m# function correctly when allocated from the pool.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mmode cockroach[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Create a variety of object types to exercise pool allocation.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE TABLE id_pool_t1 (a INT)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE TABLE id_pool_t2 (b TEXT)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE TABLE id_pool_t3 (c BOOL)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE VIEW id_pool_v1 AS SELECT * FROM id_pool_t1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE VIEW id_pool_v2 AS SELECT * FROM id_pool_t2[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW id_pool_mv1 AS SELECT count(*) FROM id_pool_t1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE DEFAULT INDEX ON id_pool_t2[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE TYPE id_pool_ty1 AS LIST (ELEMENT TYPE = INT4)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE SECRET id_pool_secret AS 'sekret'[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Verify all objects exist and have unique IDs.[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT count(DISTINCT id) FROM mz_objects WHERE name LIKE 'id_pool_%'[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m9[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Verify the objects are usable.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mINSERT INTO id_pool_t1 VALUES (1), (2), (3)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I rowsort[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT * FROM id_pool_v1[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m1[m
[32m+[m[32m2[m
[32m+[m[32m3[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT * FROM id_pool_mv1[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m3[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Drop and recreate to exercise pool across multiple rounds of allocation.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP MATERIALIZED VIEW id_pool_mv1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP VIEW id_pool_v1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP VIEW id_pool_v2[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE VIEW id_pool_v1_new AS SELECT a + 10 AS a FROM id_pool_t1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW id_pool_mv1_new AS SELECT count(*) FROM id_pool_t1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Verify the new objects work and have unique IDs.[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I rowsort[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT * FROM id_pool_v1_new[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m11[m
[32m+[m[32m12[m
[32m+[m[32m13[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT * FROM id_pool_mv1_new[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m3[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Check that recreated objects got fresh, unique IDs.[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT count(DISTINCT id) FROM mz_objects WHERE name LIKE 'id_pool_%'[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m8[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Cleanup.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP MATERIALIZED VIEW id_pool_mv1_new[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP VIEW id_pool_v1_new[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP SECRET id_pool_secret[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP TYPE id_pool_ty1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP TABLE id_pool_t3[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP TABLE id_pool_t2 CASCADE[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mDROP TABLE id_pool_t1[m

If `ids` is empty, we don't refill and return Ok(()). Then the caller (allocate_user_id) would call refill_user_id_pool(1), get Ok(()), then self.user_id_pool.allocate() would still return None (pool wasn't refilled), and .expect("ID pool just refilled") would panic. So empty allocation from catalog could cause a panic. That could be a bug - we should handle empty ids.
3. Unit tests - they use #[cfg(test)] and the skill says use #[mz_ore::test]. Let me check the test attribute.

Then allocate_user_id() would .expect() and panic. So we need to either ensure we never request 0, or handle empty response. Looking at the code: to_allocate = max(min_count, batch_size), and we're called with min_count >= 1 from allocate_user_id. So to_allocate is at least 512 (batch size). So catalog shouldn't return empty. But allocate_user_ids(count) when count is large could in theory get an empty vec from catalog in error cases? Let me check - the catalog's allocate_user_ids probably returns that many ids. So if it returns fewer than requested, we'd have a bug. I'll mention the empty-ids case as a potential edge case.

4. **Release notes**: This is an internal performance optimization (fewer persist writes during DDL). It's not a user-visible API change - it's an implementation detail. 5. **std::cmp::max**: The checklist says prefer standard library - std::cmp::max is fine. Actually they use it in the code. Good.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So the durable catalog's allocate_user_ids returns a Vec that should have exactly `amount` elements (from allocate_id). So we don't expect empty. But if amount is 0, allocate_id might return empty - let me check. Actually we have to_allocate = max(min_count, batch_size) and batch_size is 512, so we never pass 0. And when we call allocate_user_ids(count) from the coordinator, count comes from subsource_stmts.len() which could be 0. So allocate_user_ids(0) would call refill_user_id_pool(0). Then to_allocate = max(0, 512) = 512. So we'd request 512 and get 512 back. So we're good. But wait - allocate_user_ids(0) in the coordinator: we'd have remaining() < 0 is false (0 < 0), so we wouldn't refill. Then we'd call pool.allocate_many(0) which returns Some(vec![]) (from the test test_allocate_many_zero). So we'd return Ok(vec![]). That's correct.

One more edge case: what if the catalog returns fewer IDs than requested? The durable implementation allocates `amount` in one go, so it should return exactly that many. So we're good.

Let me also verify the sequencer.rs diff - the stat said 4 lines changed. I didn't see it in the diff output. Let me check if there are any other call sites that still use the old pattern.

The catalog's allocate_user_ids(amount, ts) calls allocate_id(USER_ITEM_ALLOC_KEY, amount, commit_ts). So it requests `amount` IDs. If amount is 512, we get 512 back (or an error). So we shouldn't get empty. Unless there's a bug in durable. So the only edge case would be if the catalog returned a vec with non-User variant - but that's impossible since the durable layer only creates User ids. So we're good.

**Blocking:**
- None that I see. The empty-ids case: if catalog returns empty vec, we don't refill and then .expect() panics. But the catalog implementation returns exactly `amount` ids when it succeeds. So we're OK. I could suggest adding a debug_assert or handling empty for robustness.
- Tests use #[test] instead of #[mz_ore::test]. The guide says use #[mz_ore::test]. Other tests in adapter use #[mz_ore::test]. So that's a strong suggestion.

2. Consider handling the case where catalog returns an empty or short vec (e.g. return an error instead of panicking in allocate_user_id when pool is still empty after refill). This makes the code more defensive.

We refill with first and last. So we're good. Unless allocate_user_ids returns empty - which would be a catalog bug. So we're fine. I'll add a soft suggestion to add an error if ids.is_empty() in refill to avoid the panic in allocate_user_id (so we return AdapterError instead of panicking).

Here’s the review of **commit 37d8ac144c** ([adapter] Batch-allocate user IDs to reduce persist writes during DDL):

---

# PR review: Batch-allocate user IDs (37d8ac144c)

## Summary

The change adds an in-memory **IdPool** of pre-allocated user IDs on the coordinator and uses it for DDL so that each statement doesn’t do its own persist write + timestamp oracle call. Refills are batched via a dyncfg (`USER_ID_POOL_BATCH_SIZE`, default 512). Call sites are updated to use `allocate_user_id()` / `allocate_user_ids(count)` instead of `get_catalog_write_ts()` + `catalog().allocate_user_id(ts)`. Scope is clear and matches the commit message.

---

## Blocking

**None.** No blocking issues found.

---

## Strong suggestions

1. **Use `#[mz_ore::test]` for IdPool tests**  
   The project convention is `#[mz_ore::test]` (see `coord/peek.rs`, `coord/catalog_implications.rs`, `config/params.rs`, etc.). The new `id_pool_tests` module uses plain `#[test]`. Switching to `#[mz_ore::test]` keeps the adapter crate consistent and respects the testing checklist.

2. **Handle empty refill in `refill_user_id_pool`**  
   If `catalog().allocate_user_ids(...)` ever returned an empty `ids` (e.g. catalog bug or future change), `refill_user_id_pool` would return `Ok(())` without refilling, and the next `allocate_user_id()` would hit `.expect("ID pool just refilled")` and panic. To fail in a controlled way, consider treating empty as an error, e.g. after the `if let (Some(...), Some(...))` block add something like:
   - `} else if ids.is_empty() { return Err(AdapterError::Internal("catalog returned no IDs".into())); }`  
   so callers get an `AdapterError` instead of a panic. (Right now the durable catalog guarantees a full batch on success, so this is defensive.)

---

## Nits

- **`std::cmp::max`**  
   You could use `min_count.max(u64::from(batch_size))` for consistency with common Rust style in the codebase; current code is fine.

- **Release notes**  
   This is an internal performance optimization (fewer persist writes on DDL). No user-visible API or documented behavior change, so a release note is not required by the guide.

- **SLT / unit tests**  
   I couldn’t run `bin/sqllogictest` or `cargo test` in this environment (build/toolchain). Please run `bin/sqllogictest -- test/sqllogictest/id_pool.slt` and `cargo test -p mz-adapter id_pool` locally before merge.

---

## What’s working well

- **Single semantic change**: Only user-ID allocation path is touched; cluster ID allocation is unchanged.
- **Tests**: IdPool has focused unit tests (empty, single/batch, refill, mixed, invalid range panic), and the new SLT covers tables, views, materialized views, indexes, types, secrets, and drop/recreate.
- **Docs**: `IdPool` and correctness (single-threaded coordinator, persist atomicity, multi-process) are documented; dyncfg and coordinator methods have comments.
- **Encapsulation**: Pool lives on the coordinator; call sites just use `allocate_user_id()` / `allocate_user_ids(count)`.
- **Errors**: Uses `AdapterError::Internal` with clear messages; consistent with other adapter code.
- **No stray polish**: No `dbg!`, `println!`, or commented-out code.

Once the test attribute is updated and (optionally) the empty-refill case is handled, this looks good to merge from a review perspective.

---

Author: mtabebe &lt;michael.abebe@materialize.com&gt;
Date:   Mon Mar 2 16:46:48 2026 -0500

    [adapter] Batch-allocate user IDs to reduce persist writes during DDL
    
    Problem:
    Every DDL statement (CREATE TABLE, CREATE VIEW, etc.) performs a
    persist write + timestamp oracle call to allocate a single user ID.
    This represents one extra round trip in addition to the necessary
    catalog operation for DDL.
    
    Solution:
    - Add &#96;IdPool&#96; struct with &#96;allocate()&#96;, &#96;allocate_many()&#96;, and
      &#96;refill()&#96; methods to pre-allocate user IDs in a contiguous range
    - Add &#96;USER_ID_POOL_BATCH_SIZE&#96; dyncfg (default 512) to control
      batch size
    - Add &#96;user_id_pool&#96; field to &#96;Coordinator&#96; initialized as empty
    - Add Coordinator wrapper methods &#96;allocate_user_id()&#96; and
      &#96;allocate_user_ids(count)&#96; that draw from the pool and auto-refill
      from catalog when exhausted
    - Replace all call sites that used the two-line pattern of
      &#96;get_catalog_write_ts()&#96; + &#96;catalog().allocate_user_id(ts)&#96; with
      the new pooled methods
    
    Testing:
    - New &#96;IdPool&#96; unit tests covering empty pool, single/batch
      allocation, refill, mixed allocation, and invalid range panic
    - New SLT regression test (&#96;test/sqllogictest/id_pool.slt&#96;)
      exercising tables, views, materialized views, indexes, types,
      secrets, and drop/recreate cycles

[32m+[m[32m/// A pool of pre-allocated user IDs to avoid per-DDL persist writes.[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// IDs in the range &#96;[next, upper)&#96; are available for allocation.[m
[32m+[m[32m/// When exhausted, the pool must be refilled via the catalog.[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// # Correctness[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// The pool is owned by [&#96;Coordinator&#96;], which processes all requests[m
[32m+[m[32m/// on a single-threaded event loop. Because every access requires[m
[32m+[m[32m/// &#96;&amp;mut self&#96; on the coordinator, there is no concurrent access to the[m
[32m+[m[32m/// pool — no additional synchronization is needed.[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// Global ID uniqueness is guaranteed because each refill calls[m
[32m+[m[32m/// [&#96;Catalog::allocate_user_ids&#96;], which performs a durable persist[m
[32m+[m[32m/// write that atomically reserves the entire batch before any IDs from[m
[32m+[m[32m/// it are handed out. If the process crashes after a refill but before[m
[32m+[m[32m/// all pre-allocated IDs are consumed, the unused IDs form harmless[m
[32m+[m[32m/// gaps in the sequence — user IDs are not required to be contiguous.[m
[32m+[m[32m///[m
[32m+[m[32m/// This guarantee holds even if multiple &#96;environmentd&#96; processes run[m
[32m+[m[32m/// concurrently. Each process has its own independent pool,[m
[32m+[m[32m/// but every refill goes through the shared persist-backed catalog,[m
[32m+[m[32m/// which serializes allocations across all callers. Two processes[m
[32m+[m[32m/// will therefore never receive overlapping ID ranges,[m
[32m+[m[32m/// for the same reason they could not before this pool existed.[m
[32m+[m[32m#[derive(Debug)][m
[32m+[m[32mpub(crate) struct IdPool {[m
[32m+[m[32m    next: u64,[m
[32m+[m[32m    upper: u64,[m
[32m+[m[32m}[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mimpl IdPool {[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Creates an empty pool.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn empty() -&gt; Self {[m
[32m+[m[32m        IdPool { next: 0, upper: 0 }[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Allocates a single ID from the pool, returning &#96;None&#96; if exhausted.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn allocate(&amp;mut self) -&gt; Option&lt;u64&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        if self.next &lt; self.upper {[m
[32m+[m[32m            let id = self.next;[m
[32m+[m[32m            self.next += 1;[m
[32m+[m[32m            Some(id)[m
[32m+[m[32m        } else {[m
[32m+[m[32m            None[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Allocates &#96;n&#96; consecutive IDs from the pool, returning &#96;None&#96; if[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// insufficient IDs remain.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn allocate_many(&amp;mut self, n: u64) -&gt; Option&lt;Vec&lt;u64&gt;&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        if self.remaining() &gt;= n {[m
[32m+[m[32m            let ids = (self.next..self.next + n).collect();[m
[32m+[m[32m            self.next += n;[m
[32m+[m[32m            Some(ids)[m
[32m+[m[32m        } else {[m
[32m+[m[32m            None[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Returns the number of IDs remaining in the pool.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn remaining(&amp;self) -&gt; u64 {[m
[32m+[m[32m        self.upper - self.next[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Refills the pool with the given range &#96;[next, upper)&#96;.[m
[32m+[m[32m    pub fn refill(&amp;mut self, next: u64, upper: u64) {[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert!(next &lt;= upper, "invalid pool range: {next}..{upper}");[m
[32m+[m[32m        self.next = next;[m
[32m+[m[32m        self.upper = upper;[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m[32m}[m
[32m+[m

[32m+[m[32m    /// Pre-allocated pool of user IDs to amortize persist writes across DDL operations.[m

 }[m

 impl Coordinator {[m
[35m@@ -3741,6 +3820,69 @@[m [mimpl Coordinator {[m
         Arc::make_mut(&amp;mut self.catalog)[m
     }[m
 [m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Refills the user ID pool by allocating IDs from the catalog.[m
[32m+[m[32m    ///[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Requests &#96;max(min_count, batch_size)&#96; IDs so the pool is never[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// under-filled relative to the configured batch size.[m
[32m+[m[32m    async fn refill_user_id_pool(&amp;mut self, min_count: u64) -&gt; Result&lt;(), AdapterError&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let batch_size = USER_ID_POOL_BATCH_SIZE.get(self.catalog().system_config().dyncfgs());[m
[32m+[m[32m        let to_allocate = min_count.max(u64::from(batch_size));[m
[32m+[m[32m        let id_ts = self.get_catalog_write_ts().await;[m
[32m+[m[32m        let ids = self.catalog().allocate_user_ids(to_allocate, id_ts).await?;[m
[32m+[m[32m        if let (Some((first_id, _)), Some((last_id, _))) = (ids.first(), ids.last()) {[m
[32m+[m[32m            let start = match first_id {[m
[32m+[m[32m                CatalogItemId::User(id) =&gt; *id,[m
[32m+[m[32m                other =&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m                    return Err(AdapterError::Internal(format!([m
[32m+[m[32m                        "expected User CatalogItemId, got {other:?}"[m
[32m+[m[32m                    )));[m
[32m+[m[32m                }[m
[32m+[m[32m            };[m
[32m+[m[32m            let end = match last_id {[m
[32m+[m[32m                CatalogItemId::User(id) =&gt; *id + 1, // exclusive upper bound[m
[32m+[m[32m                other =&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m                    return Err(AdapterError::Internal(format!([m
[32m+[m[32m                        "expected User CatalogItemId, got {other:?}"[m
[32m+[m[32m                    )));[m
[32m+[m[32m                }[m
[32m+[m[32m            };[m
[32m+[m[32m            self.user_id_pool.refill(start, end);[m
[32m+[m[32m        } else {[m
[32m+[m[32m            return Err(AdapterError::Internal([m
[32m+[m[32m                "catalog returned no user IDs".into(),[m
[32m+[m[32m            ));[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m        Ok(())[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Allocates a single user ID, refilling the pool from the catalog if needed.[m
[32m+[m[32m    async fn allocate_user_id(&amp;mut self) -&gt; Result&lt;(CatalogItemId, GlobalId), AdapterError&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        if let Some(id) = self.user_id_pool.allocate() {[m
[32m+[m[32m            return Ok((CatalogItemId::User(id), GlobalId::User(id)));[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m        self.refill_user_id_pool(1).await?;[m
[32m+[m[32m        let id = self.user_id_pool.allocate().expect("ID pool just refilled");[m
[32m+[m[32m        Ok((CatalogItemId::User(id), GlobalId::User(id)))[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    /// Allocates &#96;count&#96; user IDs, refilling the pool from the catalog if needed.[m
[32m+[m[32m    async fn allocate_user_ids([m
[32m+[m[32m        &amp;mut self,[m
[32m+[m[32m        count: u64,[m
[32m+[m[32m    ) -&gt; Result&lt;Vec&lt;(CatalogItemId, GlobalId)&gt;, AdapterError&gt; {[m
[32m+[m[32m        if self.user_id_pool.remaining() &lt; count {[m
[32m+[m[32m            self.refill_user_id_pool(count).await?;[m
[32m+[m[32m        }[m
[32m+[m[32m        let raw_ids = self[m
[32m+[m[32m            .user_id_pool[m
[32m+[m[32m            .allocate_many(count)[m
[32m+[m[32m            .expect("pool has enough IDs after refill");[m
[32m+[m[32m        Ok(raw_ids[m
[32m+[m[32m            .into_iter()[m
[32m+[m[32m            .map(|id| (CatalogItemId::User(id), GlobalId::User(id)))[m
[32m+[m[32m            .collect())[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
     /// Obtain a reference to the coordinator's connection context.[m
     fn connection_context(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;ConnectionContext {[m
         self.controller.connection_context()[m
[35m@@ -4510,6 +4652,7 @@[m [mpub fn serve([m

[32m+[m[32m#[cfg(test)][m
[32m+[m[32mmod id_pool_tests {[m
[32m+[m[32m    use super::IdPool;[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[mz_ore::test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_empty_pool() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 0);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), None);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(1), None);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[mz_ore::test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_allocate_single() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(10, 13);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 3);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(10));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(11));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(12));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 0);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), None);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[mz_ore::test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_allocate_many() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(100, 105);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(3), Some(vec![100, 101, 102]));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 2);[m
[32m+[m[32m        // Not enough remaining for 3 more.[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(3), None);[m
[32m+[m[32m        // But 2 works.[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(2), Some(vec![103, 104]));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 0);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[mz_ore::test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_allocate_many_zero() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(1, 5);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(0), Some(vec![]));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 4);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[mz_ore::test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_refill_resets_pool() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(0, 2);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(0));[m
[32m+[m[32m        // Refill before exhaustion replaces the range.[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(50, 52);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(50));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(51));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), None);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[mz_ore::test][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_mixed_allocate_and_allocate_many() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(0, 10);[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(0));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate_many(3), Some(vec![1, 2, 3]));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.allocate(), Some(4));[m
[32m+[m[32m        assert_eq!(pool.remaining(), 5);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m    #[mz_ore::test][m
[32m+[m[32m    #[should_panic(expected = "invalid pool range")][m
[32m+[m[32m    fn test_refill_invalid_range_panics() {[m
[32m+[m[32m        let mut pool = IdPool::empty();[m
[32m+[m[32m        pool.refill(10, 5);[m
[32m+[m[32m    }[m
[32m+[m[32m}[m

[32m+[m[32m# Regression test for pre-allocated user ID pool.[m
[32m+[m[32m# Exercises multiple DDL types to verify IDs are unique and objects[m
[32m+[m[32m# function correctly when allocated from the pool.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mmode cockroach[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Create a variety of object types to exercise pool allocation.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE TABLE id_pool_t1 (a INT)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE TABLE id_pool_t2 (b TEXT)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE TABLE id_pool_t3 (c BOOL)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE VIEW id_pool_v1 AS SELECT * FROM id_pool_t1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE VIEW id_pool_v2 AS SELECT * FROM id_pool_t2[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW id_pool_mv1 AS SELECT count(*) FROM id_pool_t1[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE DEFAULT INDEX ON id_pool_t2[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE TYPE id_pool_ty1 AS LIST (ELEMENT TYPE = INT4)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mCREATE SECRET id_pool_secret AS 'sekret'[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Verify all objects exist and have unique IDs.[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT count(DISTINCT id) FROM mz_objects WHERE name LIKE 'id_pool_%'[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m9[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32m# Verify the objects are usable.[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mstatement ok[m
[32m+[m[32mINSERT INTO id_pool_t1 VALUES (1), (2), (3)[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I rowsort[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT * FROM id_pool_v1[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m1[m
[32m+[m[32m2[m
[32m+[m[32m3[m
[32m+[m
[32m+[m[32mquery I[m
[32m+[m[32mSELECT * FROM id_pool_mv1[m
[32m+[m[32m----[m
[32m+[m[32m3[m
[32m+[m

[32m+[m

[32m+[m

[32m+[m

[32m+[m

[32m+[m

[32m+[m[32m----[m

[32m+[m[32mquery I[m

Commit **111213bef0** is the same batch-allocate user IDs change, with the earlier review feedback applied. Here’s the review.

---

# PR review: Batch-allocate user IDs (111213bef0)

## Summary

Adds an in-memory **IdPool** on the coordinator to batch-allocate user IDs for DDL, plus dyncfg `USER_ID_POOL_BATCH_SIZE` (default 512), and switches all previous per-statement `get_catalog_write_ts()` + `catalog().allocate_user_id(ts)` call sites to `allocate_user_id()` / `allocate_user_ids(count)`.

---

## Blocking

**None.**

---

## Strong suggestions

**None.** The earlier suggestions are addressed:

- **Empty refill:** `refill_user_id_pool` now returns `Err(AdapterError::Internal("catalog returned no user IDs".into()))` when `ids` is empty, so callers get a proper error instead of a panic.
- **Test attribute:** IdPool tests use `#[mz_ore::test]`, consistent with the rest of the adapter crate.

---

## Nits

- **Release notes:** Still an internal performance optimization with no user-visible API change; no release note needed.
- **Run tests before merge:** Run `bin/sqllogictest -- test/sqllogictest/id_pool.slt` and `cargo test -p mz-adapter id_pool` locally to confirm everything passes.

---

## What’s working well

- Single, clear semantic change (user-ID allocation path only).
- Good tests: IdPool unit tests (empty, single/batch, refill, mixed, invalid range) and SLT coverage for multiple DDL types and drop/recreate.
- Documentation: IdPool correctness (single-threaded coordinator, persist, multi-process) and method comments are in place.
- Defensive empty-ids handling and `AdapterError::Internal` with a short, factual message.
- `min_count.max(u64::from(batch_size))` is idiomatic.
- No stray debug or commented code.

**Verdict:** This looks good to merge once the tests are run and green. No blocking or strong suggestions; only minor nits above.

---
